methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Prusa Research is a bootstrapped robotics and manufacturing company founded by Josef Průša. The valuation analysis draws from a sector universe of 9 peers: 1 public company (ticker 277810.KQ) and 8 private entities including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics.

The peer-median method is not applicable to this analysis. The Robotics & Manufacturing sector contains 11 same-sector comparables, but filtering for stage-aligned peers (bootstrapped or adjacent-stage) or similar valuation tiers yielded fewer than three datapoints in any filtered cohort. Applying an all-sector median would introduce substantial noise given the heterogeneity of company maturity, revenue models, and capital structures within robotics. No alternative triangulation methods—such as revenue multiple, DCF, or precedent transaction analysis—have sufficient data to support quantitative conclusions.

Without a sufficiently dense and stage-aligned comparable set, this analysis cannot produce a defensible valuation range or recommendation. The key constraint is the scarcity of bootstrapped-stage robotics comparables with disclosed financials or valuations. Advancing a valuation would require either: (1) collection of non-public financing or revenue data from the peer set, (2) relaxation of stage filters to include seed/Series A robotics companies with documented terms, or (3) a shift to earnings or asset-based methods if Prusa's financial statements become available. Until such data emerges, quantitative synthesis is deferred.
